steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content: The decisive clue is the contrast: video cameras were “previously unavailable except to television broadcasters,” but affordability now gives ordinary artists access to this technology. Jenkins “recognized and took full advantage,” creating groundbreaking works—the passage shows a shift from exclusivity to accessibility.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Newfound” directly means newly obtained or recently discovered. This is the precise sense the passage needs: to highlight that Jenkins gained access to powerful technology he didn't have before, enabling his groundbreaking works. Delicate, inevitable, and habitual don't work—delicate speaks to fragility, inevitable wrongly suggests predetermined certainty, and habitual implies long-established practice rather than newness. That matches choice A — A is correct.
